source_name: NBE National Financial Inclusion Strategy II (2021-2025)
original_text: financial inclusion levels have increased markedly - from 22% to an estimated 45% in 2020
notes: Pre-Telebirr baseline (Telebirr launched May 2021). Fills the 2017-2021 gap and gives the NFIS-II starting point cited by NBE for its 70%-by-2025 target.

source_name: Determinants of Financial Inclusion in Ethiopia (RSIS Intl. Journal, using Findex 2025 microdata)
original_text: women in Ethiopia (41.62%) having notably lower ownership rates than men (56.53%) in 2024
notes: Secondary academic analysis of Findex 2024/2025 microdata (n=1,001). Implies a ~14.9pp gender gap for 2024, versus 18pp already recorded in REC_0028 (direct survey source) - a genuine cross-source discrepancy documented in data_enrichment_log.md, not resolved here.

source_name: Determinants of Financial Inclusion in Ethiopia (RSIS Intl. Journal, using Findex 2025 microdata)
original_text: women in Ethiopia (41.62%) having notably lower ownership rates than men (56.53%) in 2024
notes: Paired with REC_0035 (male). See discrepancy note there.

source_name: NBE / Ethiopia Finance Forum - Banking Sector Reforms presentation
original_text: Increased from 74.1 mn in June 2020 to 269.4 mn in June 2024
notes: New indicator: raw mobile subscription count, a leading enabler for both ACC_MOBILE_PEN and mobile-money account growth. Conference/presentation source rather than a primary regulator report, hence medium confidence.

source_name: NBE / Ethiopia Finance Forum - Banking Sector Reforms presentation
original_text: Increased from 74.1 mn in June 2020 to 269.4 mn in June 2024 (Ave. Growth of 65.1%)
notes: Paired with REC_0037. Growth trend can serve as a leading feature for both Access and Usage models.

source_name: World Bank Africa Can End Poverty blog (Global Findex 2021)
original_text: Only 42% of account holders - 20% of adults - used their accounts for digital payments
notes: IMPORTANT: this is the core World Bank Findex Usage/Access target metric named in the project brief, but it was entirely absent from the starter dataset (only proxies like P2P count and Telebirr users were present). Added as USG_DIGITAL_PAYMENT for both 2021 and 2024 (REC_0039/REC_0040) so it can be used directly as the Usage forecasting target in Task 4.

source_name: Global Findex Database 2024, as cited in project brief
notes: Figure taken from the assignment brief's own citation of Global Findex 2024/2025; not independently re-verified against the raw microdata table, hence medium (not high) confidence. See REC_0039 note.

source_name: Global Findex Database 2024, as cited in project brief
notes: Depth-of-usage indicator (wage digitization) called out explicitly in the assignment brief as a 2024 Findex indicator for Ethiopia; useful as a leading/lagging feature for the Usage model given its link to formal-sector employment.

source_name: NBE - Advancing Women's Financial Inclusion in Ethiopia
original_text: halving the account ownership gap - from 19 percentage points in 2020 to 10 percentage points by 2025
notes: Official NFIS-II gender target, complementing the existing GEN_MM_SHARE target (REC_0033). Gives the model an explicit gender-gap goal to forecast against alongside ACC_OWNERSHIP.

indicator: Bank Corporate Governance Directive (SBB/91/2024) - Board Gender Quotas
original_text: Bank Corporate Governance (SBB/91/2024): including independent directors and board gender diversity
notes: Regulatory directive requiring board gender diversity provisions at banks (part of NFIS-II gender track). Category is regulation; pillar deliberately left empty per schema rule.

indicator: National Digital Payments Strategy - Phase One Launch
original_text: the first phase strategy (NDPS 2021-2024), which significantly expanded access to digital financial services nationwide
notes: Exact launch day within 2021 not confirmed in the source (only the 2021-2024 phase window is given), so observation_date uses 2021-01-01 as a placeholder for 'start of 2021' - flagged as an approximate date, confidence set to medium accordingly.

indicator: National Digital Payments Strategy - Phase Two (NDPS 2.0) Launch
original_text: the National Bank of Ethiopia today announced the launch of Phase Two of the National Digital Payments Strategy (NDPS 2.0)
notes: Successor policy to NDPS Phase One (EVT_0012); explicitly framed by NBE as building on 2021-2023 gains (35%->46% formal access). Article publish date (Apr 3, 2025) used as the observation_date proxy for launch date.

Board-level gender quotas are an institutional enabler, not a direct driver of account-level gender gaps; effect on GEN_GAP_ACC is expected to be small and slow (via more gender-responsive product design and outreach).

EVT_0009 (NFIS-II Strategy Launch, Sep 2021) had NO impact_link in the starter dataset despite being a major national policy - gap identified in 01.ipynb. NFIS-II's own headline target is 70% account ownership by 2025 from a 45% 2020 baseline (REC_0034/REC_0031), i.e. a targeted +25pp - used here as the impact_estimate since the strategy document itself specifies the intended magnitude (empirical/target-derived rather than a generic assumption).
NFIS-II explicitly targets halving the account-ownership gender gap from 19pp (2020) to 10pp (2025) - see REC_0042 target and NBE WFISC source. impact_estimate=-9pp reflects that explicit target.

NDPS Phase Two launched Apr 2025, too recent for empirical Ethiopian evidence; magnitude set by analogy to Phase One's contribution to the 2017-2023 formal-access rise (35%->46%), scaled down since Phase Two targets usage depth rather than first-time access.

M-Pesa-EthSwitch interoperability (EVT_0007) mainly targeted in the starter data as a USAGE driver (IMP_0011/IMP_0012); adding a secondary, smaller ACCESS effect since cross-network interoperability also lowers the friction of opening a first mobile money account (fewer 'walled garden' concerns).
